body:
https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/83#issuecomment-4364641926

https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/83#issuecomment-4364664576

It's a bit too dry and not informative to users -> they are kinda like "logs" 

@sepo-agent /answer any thoughts?

comments.body:
Yes, I agree. The current comments are technically useful but read like workflow logs:

- Selection comment is built in [`.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts): `Sub-orchestrator child selected...`
- Terminal progress comment is also built there: `Result`, `Parent round`, `Next: waiting for meta orchestrator`
- Machine state is already separated into hidden markers in [`.agent/src/sub-orchestration.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/sub-orchestration.ts), so we can improve the visible copy without breaking recovery/dedupe.

I’d keep the hidden HTML markers exactly as-is, but make the visible comments more user-facing. For example:

```md
Sepo is splitting this orchestration into a focused child task.

Child task: #84
Focus: fix resumed fix-pr handoff context

I’ll let the child issue run through the normal implement/review/fix loop, then report back here before choosing the next parent step.

<!-- sepo-sub-orchestrator-child ... -->
```

And terminal report:

```md
Child task #84 finished.

Outcome: ready to ship
PR: #89
What happened: review verdict is SHIP

I’m resuming the parent orchestration now to decide whether another child task is needed.

<!-- sepo-sub-orchestrator-report ... -->
```

Implementation plan:

1. Add small formatter helpers in [`.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts) for child selection, adoption, and terminal report comments.
2. Keep markers from [`.agent/src/sub-orchestration.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/sub-orchestration.ts) unchanged and still present at the end of each comment.
3. Update expectations in [`.agent/src/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts), especially tests around child selection, adoption comments, and terminal child reports.
4. Optionally update [`.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md) to document that visible parent comments are user-facing status updates while hidden markers remain the durable state.

I’d treat this as a copy/formatting improvement only, not a behavior change. If approved, I can implement it in a small PR.

@sepo-agent /answer 
Can we create a simple tabular format?

Yes, a simple table is probably the right shape here. It keeps the status scannable without making the comment feel like raw logs.

For the child-selection comment, I’d use:

```md
Sepo is starting a focused child task for this orchestration.

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Child task | #84 |
| Focus | fix resumed fix-pr handoff context |
| Parent issue | #83 |
| Status | Running |

I’ll report back here when the child task finishes.

<!-- sepo-sub-orchestrator-child ... -->
```

For the completion report:

```md
Child task completed.

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Child task | #84 |
| PR | #89 |
| Outcome | Ready to ship |
| Parent round | 2 / 10 |
| Summary | Review verdict is SHIP |
| Next step | Resuming parent orchestration |

<!-- sepo-sub-orchestrator-report ... -->
```

I’d keep the hidden marker comments unchanged so the existing parsing/recovery logic in [`.agent/src/sub-orchestration.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/sub-orchestration.ts) keeps working. The visible formatting change should live in [`.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts), where the current parent progress comments are assembled.

Suggested implementation:

1. Add formatter helpers for parent child-selection and child-completion comments.
2. Render user-facing fields as Markdown tables.
3. Keep hidden markers at the bottom of each comment.
4. Update [`.agent/src/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts) to assert the table rows plus unchanged markers.

So yes: table for the visible status, hidden marker for state. That gives users a clearer “what happened / what’s next” view without weakening orchestration state handling.

@sepo-agent /answer 

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Child task | #84 |
| Focus | fix resumed fix-pr handoff context |
| Parent issue | #83 |
| Status | Running |

Can we transpose the table such that it will just have two rows (to reduce the vertical space taken)

Yes. A transposed table is better for compact status comments.

Example:

```md
Sepo is starting a focused child task for this orchestration.

| Child task | Focus | Parent issue | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| #84 | fix resumed fix-pr handoff context | #83 | Running |

I’ll report back here when the child task finishes.

<!-- sepo-sub-orchestrator-child ... -->
```

And for completion:

```md
Child task completed.

| Child task | PR | Outcome | Parent round | Next step |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| #84 | #89 | Ready to ship | 2 / 10 | Resuming parent orchestration |

Summary: review verdict is SHIP

<!-- sepo-sub-orchestrator-report ... -->
```

I’d use the transposed format for short, stable fields and keep longer text like `Summary` outside the table. That avoids wide, hard-to-read rows when the planner reason is verbose.

Sepo adopted this issue as a sub-orchestrator child of #104.

Stage: format-sub-orchestrator-comments
Parent round: 2

<!-- sepo-sub-orchestrator parent:104 stage:format-sub-orchestrator-comments state:done parent_round:2 -->
<!-- sepo-sub-orchestrator-adoption -->

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `orchestrate` -> `delegate_issue` (round 2/10).

agent planner selected child issue delegation: Issue #104 is a meta orchestration request over existing issues. Start with #90 because compact user-facing sub-orchestrator status formatting improves the parent/child comments that the later tasks (#95, #105, and possibly #86) build on; later children should stack on the prior open PR.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777773155338 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyNjcwNzk4MjY6b3JjaGVzdHJhdGU6MTA0OmRlbGVnYXRlX2lzc3VlOjkwOjI -->

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `orchestrate` -> `implement` (round 2/10).

manual orchestrate start on issue; dispatching implement

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777773167989 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyNjcwNzk4MjY6b3JjaGVzdHJhdGU6OTA6aW1wbGVtZW50OjkwOjI -->

**Sepo implementation finished**

- Branch: `agent/implement-issue-90/codex-25267116262`
- Pull request: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/108

Updated sub-orchestrator child selection, adoption, and completion comments to use compact transposed Markdown tables while preserving hidden markers for recovery/dedupe, and documented the visible comment contract. Verified with `npm run build`, `env -u BASE_BRANCH node --test dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js`, and `git diff --check`.

title: Better formatting the sub-orchestrator communication
